title: China-to-Peru BESS Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: Fire Safety and Installation Requirements for BESS in Peru
rows.target_requirement: Peru does not have a dedicated national BESS fire-safety standard equivalent to Korea's post-ESS-fire KFI regime. However, BESS installations in Peru are subject to general electrical installation fire-safety norms, building code requirements, civil defense (INDECI — Instituto Nacional de Defensa Civil) inspection for facilities, and project-specific fire-safety review by the project owner, insurer, and local authority. Osinergmin technical inspection may review fire-safety measures for registered generation projects. Thermal runaway mitigation evidence, gas detection or ventilation design, emergency shutdown logic, and Spanish-language emergency procedures are practical requirements for project approval and insurance.
target_standards: INDECI (Instituto Nacional de Defensa Civil) — facility fire-safety inspection requirements
Peru Reglamento Nacional de Edificaciones (RNE) — building code fire-safety provisions
Osinergmin project technical inspection for registered generation projects
Project insurer and owner fire-safety requirements
rows.china_equivalent: China commonly relies on GB 44240-2024, GB/T 36276-2023, GB/T 36558-2023, and project-specific fire-safety reviews for stationary energy storage. These documents may support technical due diligence for Peruvian project buyers and insurers but do not replace Peru INDECI, Osinergmin, local authority, or project-owner fire-safety requirements.
rows.gap: Prepare a BESS fire-safety package for Peru that includes: thermal runaway test evidence (IEC 62619 or equivalent), gas detection or ventilation design drawings, battery and PCS enclosure protection class evidence, fire suppression system design, emergency shutdown logic documentation, Spanish-language emergency procedures, and installation layout drawings showing separation distances. Present this package to the project owner, insurer, and local authority during project approval.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where INDECI inspection and local fire-safety approval apply; project owner and insurer requirements are practical project prerequisites
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory for Chinese BESS projects under GB 44240-2024 project fire-safety review; Chinese documents support due diligence but do not satisfy Peru-specific approval routes

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Prepare a Spanish-language BESS fire-safety package covering thermal runaway evidence, gas detection or ventilation, fire suppression, emergency shutdown, and emergency procedures for Peru project approval, Osinergmin inspection, INDECI review, and insurer requirements. China GB fire-safety documents support due diligence but are not substitutes.

source.publisher: Instituto Nacional de Defensa Civil (INDECI), Plataforma del Estado Peruano

rows.requirement: Peru 60 Hz Grid Validation for BESS — PCS, Transformers, and Balance of System
rows.target_requirement: Peru operates a 60 Hz electricity grid at 220 V single-phase and 380 V three-phase nominal voltages. BESS exported from China should be validated for 60 Hz operation across all electrical and electromechanical components: power conversion system (PCS), transformers, auxiliary power supplies, HVAC, relays, meters, fire protection equipment, pumps, and control systems. PCS protection settings, frequency trip windows, voltage trip windows, reconnection timing, reactive-power capability, and anti-islanding behavior must match NTCSE requirements (DS 020-97-EM) and the project's COES/SEIN interconnection approval. Factory files and factory acceptance test (FAT) records should reflect the Peru 60 Hz configuration, not a Chinese 50 Hz default.
target_standards: NTCSE — Norma Técnica de Calidad de los Servicios Eléctricos (DS 020-97-EM)
Peru 60 Hz, 220 V / 380 V nominal grid conditions
rows.china_equivalent: China domestic grid-storage equipment, PCS, and factory files are commonly prepared for 50 Hz operation under GB/T 36558-2023, GB/T 36276-2023, and GB 44240-2024. The export configuration should separately prove 60 Hz ratings, protection settings, and auxiliary-load compatibility for Peru.
rows.gap: Identify every 50 Hz-only component or setting in the China design file. Provide 60 Hz nameplate evidence, PCS type-test settings at 60 Hz, transformer and auxiliary-load ratings confirmed for 60 Hz, relay protection settings, and updated FAT / SAT procedures for Peru. Do not ship a China 50 Hz default PCS configuration.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory as an engineering and interconnection requirement for BESS connected to Peru's 60 Hz SEIN grid
mandatory_status.cn: China domestic evidence is typically 50 Hz-based; 60 Hz validation is export-market and project-specific

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Peru BESS export configurations must be proven at 60 Hz across all system components. A China 50 Hz grid-storage file is not sufficient unless the PCS and balance-of-system configuration are rated, tested, and commissioned for Peru's 60 Hz grid.

source.publisher: Organismo Supervisor de la Inversión en Energía y Minería (Osinergmin)

rows.requirement: IEC 62619 / NTP-IEC 62619 — Secondary Lithium Battery Safety for BESS
rows.target_requirement: IEC 62619:2022 specifies safety requirements for secondary lithium cells and batteries used in industrial applications, including stationary energy storage systems. INACAL has officialized the Peruvian adoption as NTP-IEC 62619:2024, based on IEC 62619:2022. Exporters should verify the current mandatory or voluntary status for the product scope with INACAL before shipment. In the absence of a mandatory national certification scheme, third-party IEC 62619 / NTP-IEC 62619:2024 test reports from an accredited laboratory are the standard evidence baseline for buyer due diligence and project approval in Peru.
target_standards: IEC 62619:2022 (secondary lithium cells and batteries for use in industrial applications — safety requirements)
NTP-IEC 62619:2024 (Peru adoption based on IEC 62619:2022 — verify mandatory status with INACAL)
rows.china_equivalent: China commonly relies on GB 44240-2024 (safety requirements for electrochemical energy storage systems) and GB/T 36276-2023 (lithium-ion batteries for stationary energy storage) for domestic BESS safety compliance. These Chinese national standards do not substitute for IEC 62619 test evidence in Peru.
rows.gap: Obtain IEC 62619:2022 / NTP-IEC 62619:2024 third-party test reports for the battery cells and modules used in the BESS export unit. GB 44240-2024 and GB/T 36276-2023 reports support technical due diligence but do not replace IEC 62619 evidence. Verify with INACAL whether NTP-IEC 62619:2024 is mandatory or voluntary for the product scope at time of export.

mandatory_status.target: NTP-IEC 62619:2024 is Peru's adoption based on IEC 62619:2022; mandatory status in Peru should be verified with INACAL at time of export
mandatory_status.cn: GB 44240-2024 is mandatory for stationary electrochemical storage systems in China; GB/T 36276-2023 is a recommended standard

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Secure IEC 62619:2022 / NTP-IEC 62619:2024 third-party test reports for the battery cells and modules in the BESS export unit. Verify NTP-IEC 62619:2024 mandatory status with INACAL. China GB 44240-2024 and GB/T 36276-2023 documents do not substitute for IEC 62619 evidence in Peru.

rows.requirement: IEC 62933 Grid Energy Storage System Performance and Safety
rows.target_requirement: The IEC 62933 series covers grid energy storage system (ESS) planning, installation, safety operation, and performance testing. IEC 62933-2-1 addresses unit parameters and test methods; IEC 62933-5-2 addresses safety requirements for grid-integrated energy storage systems. INACAL's standards development program references IEC in the energy storage space. Verify current NTP-IEC 62933 adoption status with INACAL. For utility-scale BESS projects in Peru, IEC 62933 technical documentation supports Osinergmin and COES/SEIN project technical review.
target_standards: IEC 62933-2-1 (grid integrated energy storage systems — unit parameters and test methods)
IEC 62933-5-2 (grid integrated energy storage systems — safety requirements for grid-integrated ESS)
NTP-IEC 62933 (Peru adoption status — verify with INACAL)
rows.china_equivalent: China uses GB/T 36558-2023 (general technical requirements for battery energy storage stations connected to the grid), GB/T 36276-2023, and GB 44240-2024 for grid-integrated BESS. These Chinese standards cover similar system-level performance and safety ground as IEC 62933 but are not recognized as equivalent in Peru.
rows.gap: Prepare IEC 62933 system-level technical documentation (unit parameters, performance test evidence, safety requirements compliance) for the exported BESS unit. This documentation supports project-level Osinergmin and COES/SEIN review in Peru. GB/T 36558-2023 documents support technical due diligence but are not Peru-accepted equivalents.

mandatory_status.target: IEC 62933 is the international performance and safety baseline; NTP-IEC 62933 mandatory status should be confirmed with INACAL; project-level technical review by Osinergmin/COES may require IEC 62933 evidence
mandatory_status.cn: GB/T 36558-2023 and related standards are mandatory or recommended for domestic grid-tied BESS in China

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Prepare IEC 62933 system-level technical documentation for the BESS export unit. Verify NTP-IEC 62933 adoption in Peru with INACAL. Chinese GB/T 36558-2023 documents support due diligence but do not replace IEC 62933 evidence for Peru project approval.

source.publisher: Instituto Nacional de Calidad (INACAL) — Normas Técnicas Peruanas, Electricidad

rows.requirement: UN 38.3 Transport Testing for Lithium Cells and Batteries
rows.target_requirement: Lithium cells and batteries shipped to Peru by air, sea, or land are subject to the UN Recommendations on the Transport of Dangerous Goods and IATA/IMDG/ADR dangerous goods regulations as applicable. UN 38.3 transport testing (vibration, shock, thermal cycling, altitude simulation, crush, overcharge, forced discharge) is required for all lithium cells and batteries before transport. This requirement applies regardless of whether the BESS is a complete system or battery component shipment. Peruvian customs and transport authorities apply international dangerous goods rules.
target_standards: UN Recommendations on the Transport of Dangerous Goods — Manual of Tests and Criteria, Part III, Section 38.3 (UN 38.3)
Peruvian customs dangerous goods classification requirements
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese lithium battery manufacturers obtain UN 38.3 test reports for export shipments. These UN 38.3 reports are internationally recognized and are valid for Peru import purposes if the tested cells and battery configuration match the shipped goods. The UN 38.3 report should reference the specific cell type, capacity, chemistry, and battery configuration.
china_standards: UN 38.3 test reports (held by Chinese manufacturers for export)
rows.gap: Confirm that the UN 38.3 test report covers the specific cell type, capacity, chemistry (e.g., LFP, NMC), and module/battery-pack configuration being exported. A report for a different cell size or configuration is not valid. Prepare shipping documents (transport classification, SDS, UN number, packing instructions) for Peru customs.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for all lithium battery shipments under international dangerous goods transport regulations; enforced at Peru customs
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory for Chinese export shipments; Chinese manufacturers routinely hold UN 38.3 reports

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Confirm the UN 38.3 test report covers the exact cell and battery configuration being exported to Peru. Prepare Peru-bound shipping documents for lithium dangerous goods. A generic or mismatched UN 38.3 report will not satisfy customs.

source.publisher: United Nations Economic Commission for Europe (UNECE) — UN Recommendations on the Transport of Dangerous Goods
